title: Node
description: Node renders a single interactive row for navigation, metadata lists, compact explorers, and tree-like shells with selection, quick actions, custom content, and drag-drop hooks.
intro: Use `ui-node` when you need one reusable row primitive rather than a full tree or nav container. It works well for sidebars, result rows, explorer items, and any compact structure where icon, label, selection, and row actions need to stay aligned.

featureSections.title: Basic rows
featureSections.description: The base pattern is a compact row with optional icon and selected state. This is the right starting point for simple nav or result lists.

featureSections.title: Appearance and semantic variant
featureSections.description: Use appearance for emphasis and variant for meaning. In practice this helps separate neutral navigation rows from status-heavy management rows.

featureSections.title: Density and shape
featureSections.description: Size and shape let the same row primitive fit calmer sidebars, denser explorers, or more card-like compact lists.

featureSections.title: Selection and interaction behavior
featureSections.description: Selection indicator placement, button mode, and click-select behavior change how strong the row feels as an interactive control.

featureSections.title: Custom content templates
featureSections.description: When a row needs status chips, timestamps, or other metadata, replace the default content but keep the layout compact and scannable.

featureSections.title: Quick actions
featureSections.description: Inline actions are useful in file explorers, task lists, and admin surfaces where each row can be opened, renamed, or archived.

featureSections.title: Drag source and drop target
featureSections.description: Drag and drop support is meant for lightweight move flows where a row can act as a source, a target, or both inside a larger structure.

accessibility.title: Role and row semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-node` exposes `role="treeitem"` by default and switches to `role="button"` when `asButton` is enabled. Selected and disabled state are reflected through `aria-selected` and `aria-disabled` where applicable.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard support follows the rendered role.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus to or from the row. |
| Enter / Space | Activates the row when focus is on the main node content. |
| Delete or Backspace | Not handled automatically for closable rows; wire your own removal shortcut if needed. |

accessibility.title: Custom content and row actions
accessibility.body: If you replace the default content template, preserve a clear primary label. Quick-action menus and close buttons need their own accessible names and should not make the main row purpose ambiguous.

apiSections.title: NodeComponent - ui-node
apiSections.lead: Standalone row primitive for compact navigation and list structures. It supports semantic styling, selected and disabled states, button-like behavior, custom templates, quick actions, and lightweight drag-drop events.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Corner treatment of the row container.
Optional motion direction class for selection transitions.
Renders the row with button semantics and styling hooks.
Emits selection when the row is clicked.
Enables a quick-actions region in the row.
Template rendered in the quick-actions region.
Custom payload used for drag operations.
Allows the row to act as a drop target.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits when the main row is activated.
Emits when selection is triggered by click.
Emits when the close affordance is used.
Emit while the row acts as a drop target.
{ id, label, icon?, disabled?, selected?, data?, onClick?, closable? }
Minimal row contract used by the component.

paragraphs: Use `ui-node` when you need one row primitive. If you need recursive nesting or full tree keyboard behavior, `ui-tree-node` or `ui-tree` is usually a better fit.
Custom content should stay compact. If the row turns into a mini card, it usually means the layout needs a different component.
blocks.title: Notes
